name: Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail
name_original: Orillas del Embalse de Bermejales — circular por el pantano
description: Cycle an easy 14.1-mile (22.8 km) circular route around the Bermejales Reservoir, enjoying aquamarine waters and mountain views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Embalse de los Bermejales offers varied terrain set against a backdrop of turquoise waters, pine forests, and olive groves. The region is characterized by wide expanses of water and stunning mountain views, fed by rivers and streams from the Sierra de Almijara and Sierra de Tejeda mountains. Cyclists can expect routes with moderate elevation gains, providing both scenic beauty and engaging challenges. The natural park Sierras Tejeda, Almijara, and Alhama further defines the dramatic landscape. Best no traffic road cycling routes around Embalse de los Bermejales The most popular no traffic road cycling route is Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail, an easy 14.1 miles (22.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 5 minutes to complete, offering continuous views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is View of Alhama de Granada – Pantaneta de Alhama loop from Arenas del Rey, a moderate 23.3 miles (37.5 km) path. This route features a mix of rolling hills and scenic vistas towards Alhama de Granada. Local road cyclists also love the View of Embalse de Bermejales loop from Arenas del Rey, a 47.2 miles (76.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes with significant climbs, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around Embalse de los Bermejales is defined by reservoir perimeters, olive groves, and mountain backdrops, with options available for easy rides and more challenging routes with substantial elevation gain. The routes in Embalse de los Bermejales are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Embalse de los Bermejales's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many no-traffic road cycling routes are available around Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: There are several dedicated no-traffic road cycling routes around Embalse de los Bermejales. Our guide features 3 carefully selected routes, ranging from easy to difficult, ensuring options for various skill levels.

Q: What are the typical lengths and difficulty levels of the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: The routes vary in length and challenge. For an easy ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983157298/bermejales-reservoir-perimeter-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 22.7 km. Moderate options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38162384" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alhama de Granada – Pantaneta de Alhama loop from Arenas del Rey&lt;/a&gt; at 37.4 km. For a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10307537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Bermejales loop from Arenas del Rey&lt;/a&gt; covers approximately 76 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area offers routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983157298/bermejales-reservoir-perimeter-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, moderate-length option that provides beautiful views without excessive difficulty, making it a good choice for a family outing.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a no-traffic road cycling route?
A: Parking is generally available in nearby municipalities that serve as starting points for circular routes, such as Fornes and Arenas del Rey. You can also find parking near the reservoir itself, for example, at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3305216" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Five-Branch Pine&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see along these routes?
A: You'll cycle through diverse and picturesque landscapes. Expect stunning views of the turquoise waters of Embalse de los Bermejales, surrounded by pine forests, olive groves, and almond trees. The routes often feature the dramatic backdrop of the Sierra de Almijara and Sierra de Tejeda mountains.

Q: Are there any notable viewpoints along the no-traffic road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes offer panoramic vistas of the reservoir and surrounding mountains. A particularly recommended spot is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5427076" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Yeseras&lt;/a&gt;, which provides breathtaking views of the water and the natural park.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I visit near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3159541" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de los Bermejales&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient megalithic burial site. The nearby spa town of Alhama de Granada, known for its historic Roman baths, is also just a short distance away and can be incorporated into longer rides, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40018422" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Alhama de Granada – View of the Alhama Gorge loop from Alhama de Granada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling around Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: The region is excellent for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Even winter can be suitable, as the Mediterranean climate often provides mild conditions, allowing for tranquil, traffic-free rides.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many of the no-traffic road cycling routes around Embalse de los Bermejales are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983157298/bermejales-reservoir-perimeter-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bermejales Reservoir Perimeter Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10307537" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Embalse de Bermejales loop from Arenas del Rey&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Embalse de los Bermejales?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.875 stars. Reviewers often praise the tranquil cycling experience away from heavy traffic, the stunning views of the turquoise reservoir, and the diverse terrain that caters to various fitness levels.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the routes?
A: While specific cafes might not be directly on every no-traffic section, the small villages surrounding the reservoir, such as Bermejales and Arenas del Rey, offer quaint and welcoming experiences with options for refreshments. The village of Pantano de Los Bermejales, near the dam, also caters to tourists.
tips: Road that comes from Cortijo de la Loma and goes up to the road that goes in the direction of Fornes. From this point you can see the turquoise waters generated by the Bermejales Reservoir.
Bermejales Reservoir area from where you can cross the dam. From this point you can see the extent of the swamp and how it gets big from the same town of Pantano de los Bermejales.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This easy racebike route around the Banks of the Bermejales Reservoir is approximately 22.8 km long with about 294 meters of elevation gain. It typically takes around 1 hour and 5 minutes to complete.

faqs.question: Is this circular route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific circular route is considered easy and is suitable for almost any fitness level. It features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it a good option for beginners and families looking for a scenic ride. However, be aware that other routes in the broader Bermejales area can be more challenging.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Banks of the Bermejales Reservoir circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is predominantly well-paved, offering a smooth ride for racebikes. You can expect undulating roads with occasional short climbs. While generally easy, some accounts mention a short stretch on a cobbled road and a poorly surfaced section through a wooded area near a campsite, which might require a bit more attention.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers stunning scenery, including aquamarine waters of the Embalse de Bermejales framed by the Almijara mountains. You'll pass through rolling hills and olive groves, with potential distant views of Granada and the Sierra Nevada. Don't miss the View of Embalse de Bermejales.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the cycling trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near the reservoir, particularly around the main access points or recreational areas. Specific parking details can vary, so it's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle around the Bermejales Reservoir?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle is typically during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder. Summers can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable. Winters are generally mild but can be cooler and wetter.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the cycling route?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily for cycling, dogs are generally allowed in the natural park area, but they should always be kept on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or specific permits required for cycling this route within the Sierra de Tejeda, Almijara y Alhama Natural Park. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route around the reservoir intersects with several other paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Sendero perimetral del Pantano de los Bermejales, Camino de la Cuesta del Algar, and briefly with the Transandalus, Tajos de los Bermejales, Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), Camino de Napoleón, and Camino Romero.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is primarily natural, there are often small villages or recreational areas near the reservoir where you might find cafes or places to refill water, especially during peak seasons. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks.

faqs.question: What other activities can be done at Embalse de Bermejales?
faqs.answer: The Embalse de Bermejales is popular for various outdoor activities beyond cycling. During summer months, it's a great spot for swimming, kayaking, and picnics. The surrounding area also offers opportunities for hiking and enjoying the natural beauty of the park.
